items.title: Hedge Fund Sells $25.6 Million in Paramount Group Stock as Rithm's $1.6 Billion Deal Looms
items.author: Jonathan Ponciano
items.description: Boston-based Solel Partners fully exited its stake in Paramount Group, selling 4.2 million shares for $25.6 million in Q3, likely due to the pending Rithm Capital acquisition and limited future upside.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock performance is tied to pending Rithm Capital acquisition at $6.60 per share, with limited future growth potential
Announced acquisition of Paramount Group, indicating strategic investment opportunity

items.title: Why One Fund Just Invested $21 Million in This Once High-Flying Software Stock
items.author: Jonathan Ponciano
items.description: Boston-based Solel Partners initiated a $21.2 million position in Braze stock during Q3, acquiring 745,900 shares despite the company's stock trading near multi-year lows. The investment suggests potential value in Braze's growing customer base and AI-driven product investments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company shows promising revenue growth (24% in latest quarter) but is not yet consistently profitable, with ongoing GAAP losses and weakening free cash flow
Top holding in Solel Partners' portfolio, representing 13.7% of assets under management
Significant portfolio holding representing 10.4% of assets under management
